name: checklist-full-v5
description: Reliance-grade audit checklist — 99 rules across 10 sections. Source-traceable to Model_Checklist_V2_17_6_2026.xlsx (296 controls). Covers automatable subset only — diagnostics accelerate review but do not replace human judgment, source-document review or commercial omission testing.
source_standard: ICAEW FMC + FAST + PwC GMG + ASIC RG170 + ASAE3450
automation_note: This checklist covers the automatable subset of the gold master. Phases 1 (scope), 2 (evidence), 5 (cell-by-cell), 11 (omission), and 12 (reporting) require human review and are excluded.

tier1.label: No Excel formula errors anywhere in workbook
tier1.fix_instruction: Investigate and correct at source. Never replace with a hardcoded value or mask with IFERROR. Identify the root cause: broken link, missing reference, division by zero, or lookup failure.

tier1.label: Minimum sheet structure present
tier1.fix_instruction: File must contain at least 2 recognisable financial model sheets. Verify the correct file was uploaded.

tier1.label: Model Issues tab is empty
tier1.fix_instruction: Resolve all open items in the Model Issues tab before submitting for reliance review.

tier1.label: Balance sheet check sheet exists
tier1.fix_instruction: Add a balance sheet check row showing Assets minus Liabilities minus Equity equals zero for every period.

tier1.label: Cash flow reconciliation sheet exists
tier1.fix_instruction: Add a cash reconciliation check row: change in balance sheet cash equals operating plus investing plus financing cash flows.

tier1.label: No negative PP&E values
tier1.fix_instruction: Gross PP&E cannot be negative. Review the asset roll-forward on the AFS sheet.

tier1.label: No uncontrolled external workbook links
tier1.fix_instruction: Remove or document all external workbook links. External links create hidden dependencies that can silently break when source files move or change.

tier1.label: No negative cash balance in any period
tier1.fix_instruction: Unexplained negative cash balance found. Any funding gap must be explicitly flagged and sized. Review funding waterfall and debt availability.

tier1.label: Actuals and forecast separated by explicit flags
tier1.fix_instruction: Actuals and forecast must be separated by explicit actual/forecast flags rather than manual overwriting of formulas. Add a timing or flag row controlled by a single switch on the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Model purpose and audience documented
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a cover sheet or README documenting model purpose, audience, reliance level, and key limitations.

tier2.label: FAST principles applied — no opaque constructs
tier2.fix_instruction: Simplify flagged formula. Break into intermediate steps. Remove opaque constructs. No formula should require the builder to explain it verbally.

tier2.label: Inputs centralised — no business assumptions hard-coded in calculation sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Move hard-coded business assumptions to the Inputs sheet and replace with cell references.

tier2.label: No duplicated calculation logic across sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Remove duplicate calculation blocks. Each calculation should exist once and be referenced elsewhere.

tier2.label: No mixed monthly and quarterly logic on same sheet
tier2.fix_instruction: Annual views must be explicit aggregations only. No independent annual logic that can diverge from the monthly or quarterly engine.

tier2.label: Every key output traceable to live formulas — no pasted values
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace pasted values on dashboard and output sheets with live cell references linking to calculation sheets. Verify all outputs update correctly when assumptions change.

tier2.label: Every key assumption has reasonable support
tier2.fix_instruction: Document the basis for each material assumption on the Inputs sheet. Assumptions must not be merely management targets or balancing numbers without support.

tier2.label: No critical calculations on output sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Move calculation logic to a supporting schedule sheet and replace with a reference link on the output sheet.

tier2.label: Single master timeline drives all time-series sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Link all sheet timelines to the master timeline on the Inputs or Timing sheet. Remove any independent timeline logic.

tier2.label: No hidden sheets affecting results
tier2.fix_instruction: Unhide all sheets and confirm none contain material calculations affecting outputs without disclosure.

tier2.label: Model extendable without formula rewrites
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm that adding extra periods, assets, or debt facilities can be done by copying existing blocks without rewriting logic.

tier2.label: No volatile functions in core model engine
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace OFFSET and INDIRECT functions in core calculation areas with static references or structured table logic.

tier2.label: Logic flows left-to-right, top-to-bottom — no backward dependencies
tier2.fix_instruction: Restructure sheets so logic flows left-to-right across tabs and top-to-bottom within tabs. Backward dependencies obscure auditability and increase error risk.

tier2.label: Live model map exists showing every sheet and its purpose
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a model map tab or cover sheet section listing every worksheet, its purpose, owner, input/output status, and criticality.

tier2.label: Opening forecast balance sheet equals latest actual closing balance sheet
tier2.fix_instruction: The opening forecast balance sheet must equal the latest actual closing balance sheet after all reclassifications. Review the actuals cut-off date and opening balance links.

tier2.label: Period flags are mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive
tier2.fix_instruction: Period flags (construction, operations, actuals, forecast etc.) must be mutually exclusive and reconcile to total period count. No period should be uncategorised or double-counted.

tier2.label: All inputs on dedicated sheets with labels, units, and source
tier2.fix_instruction: Consolidate all inputs to the Inputs sheet. Add labels, units, sign conventions, source references, and last update date.

tier2.label: No business assumptions hard-coded in formula areas
tier2.fix_instruction: Move all business assumptions (price, volume, margin, tax rate, debt terms, capex) to the Inputs sheet and replace formula constants with cell references.

tier2.label: Historical data reconciles to source documents
tier2.fix_instruction: Add reconciliation tables showing historical financial statements tie to source documents (audited FS, management accounts).

tier2.label: Historical data is current and not stale
tier2.fix_instruction: Update historical data to the most recent available period. Flag any data that is outdated relative to the model start date.

tier2.label: Key assumptions entered once and referenced consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise all key assumptions on the Inputs sheet and replace all instances elsewhere with a single cell reference.

tier2.label: Every input has at least one live dependent
tier2.fix_instruction: Identify and remove or quarantine inputs with no live dependents. Orphaned inputs mislead users into thinking they are changing the model when they are not.

tier2.label: Annual assumptions correctly converted to monthly or quarterly
tier2.fix_instruction: Verify annual assumptions are converted to monthly or quarterly using correct compounding or simple pro-rating logic. Simple division by 12 is wrong for compounding rates.

tier2.label: Nominal and real assumptions not mixed without conversion
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm all assumptions are consistently nominal or real. If mixing, add an explicit inflation conversion step and document the basis.

tier2.label: GST-inclusive and exclusive amounts not mixed in same calculation stream
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm the model is consistently GST-inclusive or GST-exclusive. Document the chosen treatment on the Inputs sheet. Mixing treatments overstates or understates margins.

tier2.label: Scenario-controlled inputs linked to scenario table — not independently set
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise scenario selection to a single input cell on the Inputs or Dashboard sheet. Link all scenario-dependent assumptions through consistent INDEX or IF logic.

tier2.label: Single formula per row on all time-series
tier2.fix_instruction: Standardise the formula across each flagged row. Check for mid-row edits, broken copy-paste, or anchoring inconsistencies.

tier2.label: Consistent sign convention throughout workbook
tier2.fix_instruction: Review and standardise sign conventions. Document the chosen convention on the Inputs sheet and apply consistently across all sheets.

tier2.label: No unintended circular references
tier2.fix_instruction: Document any intentional circular references on the Inputs sheet. Confirm they converge reliably and can be switched off for audit.

tier2.label: Unit consistency and conversion checks
tier2.fix_instruction: Check for unit conversion errors (monthly vs annual rates, MW vs kW, $000 vs $m etc.). Add explicit unit labels to all input rows.

tier2.label: Lookup function integrity
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, and XLOOKUP functions. Confirm exact-match settings, range references are correct, and missing value handling is explicit.

tier2.label: IFERROR and error suppression does not hide real calculation defects
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all IFERROR, IFNA, and ISERROR usage. Confirm each is suppressing an expected and documented condition, not hiding a real calculation defect.

tier2.label: SUM and SUMIFS ranges cover full time-series without gaps or double counts
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all SUM/SUMIFS ranges for partial range errors, subtotal double-counts, omitted inserted rows, and inconsistent start/end periods.

tier2.label: IRR and NPV formulas use correct timing, sign convention, and dates
tier2.fix_instruction: Review every IRR/XIRR/NPV/XNPV formula for correct timing convention, sign convention, terminal value inclusion, and irregular cash flow dates.

tier2.label: MIN and MAX constraints reflect commercial terms — not masking problems
tier2.fix_instruction: Review every MIN/MAX constraint and confirm the cap or floor reflects documented commercial terms rather than masking a calculation problem.

tier2.label: Balance sheet balances for every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Balance sheet does not balance — fatal error. Review equity roll-forward, retained earnings, and debt closing balances on AFS, IFS, and Debt sheets.

tier2.label: Change in cash equals net cash flow for every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Cash flow does not reconcile to balance sheet. Review all three cash flow sections for missing items or double-counted flows.

tier2.label: Net income flows through to retained earnings
tier2.fix_instruction: Retained earnings roll-forward does not reconcile. Review the link from the IFS net profit line to the retained earnings opening balance.

tier2.label: Equity movements reconcile between balance sheet and cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Equity movement appears in balance sheet but not in financing cash flow or vice versa. Reconcile the equity schedule to the financing activities section.

tier2.label: Working capital movements reconcile between balance sheet and operating cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Working capital movement in balance sheet does not match operating cash flow. Review receivables, payables, and inventory links.

tier2.label: No logically impossible balance sheet positions
tier2.fix_instruction: Logically impossible balance sheet value found. Review the flagged line item and correct the formula or assumption driving the impossible value.

tier2.label: Income tax calculation is reconcilable
tier2.fix_instruction: Tax calculation appears disconnected from taxable profits. Review the tax schedule and verify the effective rate reconciliation.

tier2.label: Deferred tax treatment disclosed
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a deferred tax schedule reconciling temporary differences, or document the simplification explicitly on the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: No P&L, balance sheet or cash flow line is a plug
tier2.fix_instruction: A plug is a line item used only to force a check to zero rather than being calculated from first principles. Identify and remove all plugs. Understand and fix the underlying cause of the imbalance.

tier2.label: Annual statements reconcile to monthly or quarterly detail
tier2.fix_instruction: Annual statements must reconcile to monthly or quarterly detail. Annual totals must not sum balance-sheet accounts across periods — only flow items should be summed.

tier2.label: Fixed assets roll from opening NBV through additions, disposals and depreciation
tier2.fix_instruction: Fixed asset roll-forward must close: opening NBV + additions - disposals at NBV - depreciation = closing NBV. Review the PP&E schedule on the AFS sheet.

tier2.label: Debt roll-forward identity holds for each facility
tier2.fix_instruction: Debt roll-forward does not balance. Review drawdown, repayment, capitalised interest, FX effects, and fee amortisation links on the Debt sheet.

tier2.label: Interest calculated from referenced rates — no hard-coded rates
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace any hard-coded interest rates in the Debt sheet with references to the Inputs sheet base rate and margin assumptions.

tier2.label: Covenant calculations follow documented definitions
tier2.fix_instruction: Review covenant calculation definitions against the term sheet. Update formulas to match the documented definitions exactly.

tier2.label: Capex phasing feeds into PP&E, balance sheet and investing cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the Capex schedule links to both the AFS balance sheet and the Cons investing activities section.

tier2.label: Cash waterfall sequence is valid
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the cash waterfall sequence: revenue → O&M → taxes → debt service → reserves → distributions. Confirm each step is correctly implemented.

tier2.label: Cash sweep and lock-up mechanisms operate correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Review cash sweep and lock-up logic. Under covenant breach, distributions should be blocked and excess cash directed to debt pay-down or trapped in reserves per the term sheet.

tier2.label: DSRA and reserve accounts reconcile correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Review DSRA and other reserve accounts. Confirm they correctly size, fund, release, and replenish, tying to both cash flow and balance sheet.

tier2.label: Ownership percentages sum to 100% in every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Ownership percentages must sum to exactly 100% in every period. Review equity schedule for dilution events, option exercises, or rounding errors.

tier2.label: Preferred return accrues at correct rate with correct compounding
tier2.fix_instruction: Verify preferred return or coupon accrues on the correct base, at the correct rate, with correct compounding convention and payment timing as per shareholder documents.

tier2.label: Distributions cannot exceed available cash after all senior obligations
tier2.fix_instruction: Distributions must not exceed available cash after tax, reserves, senior debt service, and restricted cash requirements are satisfied.

tier2.label: Capitalised, cash and accrued interest separated — not double counted
tier2.fix_instruction: Check capitalised interest, cash interest, and accrued interest are separately modelled and reconcile without double counting. Capitalised interest should flow to the debt balance, not to the P&L interest line.

tier2.label: Revenue equals price times volume consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Add explicit price times volume calculations on the Ops sheet referencing price inputs from the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Production volumes within installed capacity
tier2.fix_instruction: Review production volume assumptions. Volumes must not exceed modelled capacity in any period without a corresponding capacity expansion event.

tier2.label: Fixed versus variable cost classification is sensible
tier2.fix_instruction: Review cost classification. Variable costs should move with volume in a plausible way. Fixed costs should not spike without a documented driver.

tier2.label: Margins plausible relative to history and industry norms
tier2.fix_instruction: Review margin assumptions on the Inputs sheet. Compare against historical periods and industry benchmarks for the specific industry and operation type.

tier2.label: IRR, NPV and valuation metrics within plausible range
tier2.fix_instruction: Review discount rate assumptions and cash flow profile. Compare return metrics against risk and market practice for the specific project type.

tier2.label: Revenue recognition separated from cash receipts, deposits, and settlements
tier2.fix_instruction: Revenue recognition timing must be separated from cash receipt timing. Customer deposits, billings, and settlement proceeds are not revenue until earned.

tier2.label: Bad debts, rebates, vacancy, churn and ramp-up included where relevant
tier2.fix_instruction: Check that bad debts, rebates, incentives, vacancy, churn, downtime, and ramp-up adjustments are included where commercially relevant. Omitting these overstates revenue.

tier2.label: Payroll uses headcount, salary, super, payroll tax and annual escalation
tier2.fix_instruction: Payroll must be built from headcount times salary, including commencement date, bonus, superannuation, payroll tax, leave provisions, and annual escalation assumptions.

tier2.label: Capex and maintenance capex separated from operating costs
tier2.fix_instruction: Capex and maintenance capex must be separated from operating costs. Mixing them distorts EBITDA, depreciation, and investing cash flows.

tier2.label: Scenario engine centrally controlled
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise scenario selection to a single input cell on the Inputs or Dashboard sheet. Link all scenario-dependent assumptions through consistent INDEX or IF logic.

tier2.label: Core sensitivities produce directionally correct responses
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the link between the sensitivity input and the affected output. A counter-intuitive result usually indicates a mis-linked formula or reversed sign convention.

tier2.label: Zero revenue stress test passes without model errors
tier2.fix_instruction: Setting main revenue drivers to zero should produce zero revenue, logical shutdown of variable costs, and a structurally intact model with no error explosions.

tier2.label: Date and period edge cases do not break model
tier2.fix_instruction: Test partial periods and period boundary shifts. Misaligned formulas or broken logic in edge cases must be corrected.

tier2.label: Downside cases do not suppress funding gaps or covenant breaches
tier2.fix_instruction: Review downside scenario outputs. Funding gaps, covenant breaches, negative cash, and distribution locks must surface explicitly — not be silently suppressed by MIN(0) or IFERROR logic.

tier2.label: Sensitivity outputs reconcile to base when sensitivity variable equals base assumption
tier2.fix_instruction: When a sensitivity variable is set to equal the base assumption, the sensitivity output must reconcile exactly to the base model output. Any discrepancy indicates the sensitivity is not wired correctly.

tier2.label: No debt available stress test — equity funding gap logic works correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Setting debt availability to zero should produce a visible equity or funding gap, not a silent balance that hides the shortfall. Review funding hierarchy logic.

tier2.label: Delayed start date test — timing shifts costs, interest and revenue correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Delaying the project start date should shift operating costs, capex, capitalised interest, and revenue activation in the same direction. Review period flag and switch logic.

tier2.label: Dedicated checks sheet aggregates all critical tests
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a dedicated Checks sheet aggregating all critical model integrity tests with clear PASS or FAIL flags, sheet references, and cell references for each test.

tier2.label: Change log exists with version history
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a change log tab or section on the cover sheet. Record date, editor, description of changes, and version tag for each material edit.

tier2.label: Independent review evidence recorded
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a note on the cover sheet or change log indicating that a peer or independent review has been conducted, with date and scope noted.

tier2.label: Valuation and return metrics calculated from live cash flows — not pasted values
tier2.fix_instruction: IRR, NPV, equity multiple, and all return metrics must be calculated directly from live investor or project cash flows — not from pasted or manually entered summary values.

tier2.label: Dashboard presents core KPIs and links to calculations
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace any pasted values on the Dashboard with live cell references. Verify all charts and tables update correctly under scenario changes.

tier2.label: Cover sheet documents model purpose, structure and limitations
tier2.fix_instruction: Add or complete the cover sheet with model purpose, version, author, date, structure overview, scenario controls, key assumptions, known limitations, and instructions for running checks.

tier2.label: Output sheets readable on screen and in print
tier2.fix_instruction: Set consistent print areas, fonts, and column widths. Remove cut-off tables. Ensure dashboard is readable without horizontal scrolling.

tier2.label: Simplifications and known limitations explicitly documented
tier2.fix_instruction: Document all known simplifications on the cover sheet or Inputs sheet (e.g. deferred tax excluded, leases simplified, minor revenue streams excluded). A model owner representation of completeness is best practice.
